completed_steps.description: Establish a compatible Next.js foundation for notepad application. Create the root layout, global styling primitives, and any package or config updates required for a clean install and production build. Make the app shell responsive and production-ready so later feature work lands on stable scaffolding.

Implement the main notepad application experience in the primary page and supporting components. Translate the task requirements into concrete UI sections, data presentation, and user interactions instead of generic placeholder content. Ensure the page reflects the task's actual workflows, edge states, and visual hierarchy.

Harden the implementation for autonomous delivery. Add any supporting helpers, polish incomplete states, and remove fragile dependencies or imports that would break npm install or npm run build. Finish with production-focused cleanup so the tester sees clear progress and a stable build.

cached_blueprint:
Task title: Build a Notepad Application
Task description: Create a simple notepad application with the following features:
- Create, open, edit, and save text files
- Basic text editing functionality (cut, copy, paste, undo, redo)
- Support for multiple file formats (txt, rtf)
- Find and replace functionality
- Word wrap option
- Status bar showing line and column numbers
- Clean and intuitive user interface

The application should be built using a modern programming language and framework. Include proper error handling and file management. The code should be well-documented with comments explaining key functionality.
Requirements: - User can create new text documents
- User can open existing text files
- User can save and save-as documents
- Cut, copy, paste functionality works correctly
- Undo and redo operations function properly
- Find and replace feature implemented
- Word wrap toggle available
- Status bar displays cursor position
- Application handles file errors gracefully
- Code is well-commented and organized
Project type: nextjs
Scaffold command: npx create-next-app@latest ./ --typescript --tailwind --eslint --app --no-src-dir --import-alias @/* --yes --force --no-git --skip-install

Implementation blueprint:
Step 1: Establish a compatible Next.js foundation for notepad application. Create the root layout, global styling primitives, and any package or config updates required for a clean install and production build. Make the app shell responsive and production-ready so later feature work lands on stable scaffolding.
Files: app/layout.tsx, app/globals.css

Step 2: Implement the main notepad application experience in the primary page and supporting components. Translate the task requirements into concrete UI sections, data presentation, and user interactions instead of generic placeholder content. Ensure the page reflects the task's actual workflows, edge states, and visual hierarchy.
Files: app/page.tsx, components/MainExperience.tsx

Step 3: Harden the implementation for autonomous delivery. Add any supporting helpers, polish incomplete states, and remove fragile dependencies or imports that would break npm install or npm run build. Finish with production-focused cleanup so the tester sees clear progress and a stable build.
Files: components/StatusPanel.tsx, lib/mock-data.ts

Command: npm run build
Diagnosis: Missing dependency or import: `@tailwindcss/oxide-linux-x64-gnu`. Require stack: Add the package or remove the broken import, then rebuild.
Preferred fix: remove unstable Turbopack-only build flags, align Next.js/PostCSS/Tailwind config, or fall back to the stable webpack build path.
Preferred fix: install the missing dependency only if it is truly required; otherwise remove the import and implement the feature without that package.
Do not give up. Return to coding, make concrete file changes, and rerun verification.
